name: Chalk and the Rust Next-Generation Trait Solver
summary: Chalk lowers Rust trait information into logical predicates and uses a Prolog-engine-style solver, embeddable in rustc and rust-analyzer. The next-generation trait solver in rustc reframes trait checking as proving goals under a parameter environment, returning success/ambiguity/error plus constraints; targeted for stabilization in 2025-2026.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Chalk lowers Rust trait information into logical predicates and uses a solver similar to a Prolog engine, while remaining embeddable in hosts such as rustc and rust-analyzer.

Rust's next-generation trait solver frames trait checking as proving goals under a parameter environment, returning success, ambiguity, or error plus constraints. Status (as of 2026-04): a flagship 2025–2026 Rust project goal targets stabilization of `-Znext-solver=globally`, replacing the existing trait solver implementation entirely; LWN's March 2026 coverage describes the rewrite as simplifying future trait-system changes, fixing several long-standing soundness bugs, and improving compile times on trait-heavy code.

sources.label: Rustc dev guide — next-gen trait solving

sections.title: Logic-programming shape and projection equality
sections.content:
Chalk's input language is Horn-clause-like: traits become predicates, impls become rules, where-clauses become goals. The solver answers questions like "is `i32: Iterator`?" by searching the rules for a derivation. Associated-type projections (`<I as Iterator>::Item`) get a normalization predicate that can be unified incrementally rather than requiring an answer up front.

This logic-programming shape is what makes Chalk extensible: new trait features (associated constants, associated types with where-clauses, GATs) plug in as additional rule schemas, without restructuring the solver itself. Rust-analyzer uses Chalk to provide hover-time trait resolution that matches what rustc produces, so editor diagnostics agree with batch compilation.

sections.title: Why the next-generation solver matters

sources.label: Rustc dev guide — next-generation trait solving
